titlestmt.titleproper: Institute for Defense and Disarmament Studies Records8588
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-10-29 18:44:36 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Institute for Defense and Disarmament Studies Records
did.abstract: Collection includes material relating to the Nuclear Weapons Freeze Campaign (NWFC), reports, correspondence, Democratic National Committee material, Political Action Committee (PAC) material, Randy Forsberg publications and speeches and a print copy of her PhD Thesis, IDDS publications, audio/video material, print copies of the Arms Control Reporter, other administrations files.
did.physdesc: Audio recordings, computer media, videos, correspondence, printed materials, newspapers.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Institute for Defense and Disarmament Studies (IDDS) was a nonprofit center, founded by Dr. Randall Caroline Forsberg, that studied global military policies, arms holdings, production and trade, arms control and peace-building efforts. It also ran educational programs on current and alternative policies.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes material relating to the Nuclear Weapons Freeze Campaign (NWFC), reports, correspondence, Democratic National Committee material, Political Action Committee (PAC) material, Randy Forsberg publications and speeches and a print copy of her PhD Thesis, IDDS publications, audio/video material, print copies of the Arms Control Reporter, other administrations files.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Institute for Defense and Disarmament Studies Records, #8588. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also the volume Toward a Theory of Peace : The Role of Moral Beliefs, the print version of Randall Forsberg's PhD dissertation with an introduction by Matthew Evangelista and Neta C. Crawford. Available online through the Cornell University Press. See also the Arms Control Reporter publication, JX1974.7.A73.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual material that has been digitized: V-10293, V11004

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: The Arms Control Reporter, 1982-2001, 2006, and 2007, from Boxes 22 and 25-27 have been cataloged separatley as call number JX1974.7 .A73 +. Box 25, Box 26 and Box 27 have been removed from the collection.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: The Arms Control Reporter volumes are available online here: https://catalog.hathitrust.org/Record/000068634

did.unittitle: Mixed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains grouping of folders: newspapers clippings and articles (nuclear warfare, military budgets, civil defense, missile defense, nuclear proliferation, arms race overview); research about different topics; some speeches by Randy Forsberg

did.unittitle: Freeze materials

scopecontent.p: Contains grouping of folders; includes original IDDS Brochures 1981 – Freeze and Beyond: Confining the Military Defense as Route to Disarmament - original world policy summary, and papers by Matthew Evangelista, Mark Niedergang, and Forsberg

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: mold--opened cartridge and cleaned tape; condition poor, EP recording 6 hours long, mostly t.v. programming (e.g. Murder She Wrote), edited digitized video to only include pertinent material
